questions.question: Which of these questions does an 'Adverb Clause of Time' typically answer?
questions.explanation: Adverb clauses of time provide information about when the action in the main clause occurs [1].

questions.question: Identify the subordinator in the sentence: 'It always rains after I have watered the plants.'
questions.explanation: 'After' is the subordinator introducing the adverb clause of time [1, 2].

questions.question: What type of adverb clause is found in: 'I couldn’t go to the fair because I was busy'?
questions.explanation: The clause 'because I was busy' explains the reason for the main action [1, 2].

questions.question: Identify the type of adverb clause: 'Do it as your teacher showed you.'
questions.explanation: The clause 'as your teacher showed you' tells us how (the manner) the action should be done [1, 2].

questions.question: Which marker is used to introduce an Adverb Clause of Purpose?
questions.explanation: 'So that' is a standard subordinator for expressing purpose [2].

questions.question: In the sentence 'If you heat ice, it melts', what does the conditional clause suggest?
questions.explanation: Scientific or universal truths use the present simple in both clauses, known as zero conditional [3].

questions.question: Identify the adverb clause type (Activity 2): 'Where there is no vision, the people suffer.'
questions.explanation: The clause introduced by 'where' indicates place [2, 4].

questions.question: Complete the first conditional: 'If you work hard, you ________ a first division.'
questions.explanation: The first conditional uses the present simple in the if-clause and 'will/shall' in the main clause [3, 5].

questions.question: Identify the type (Activity 2): 'We went out to play as the weather became better.'
questions.explanation: In this context, 'as' is used to show time, meaning 'when' [2, 4].

questions.question: What tense is used in the main clause of a second (imaginary) conditional?
questions.explanation: Second conditionals use modal pasts like 'would', 'could', or 'might' [5].

questions.question: Identify the error in punctuation: 'If I were you I would take the offer.'

options.B: There should be a comma after 'you'
options.C: There should be a comma after 'I'

questions.explanation: If the adverb clause comes at the beginning, it must be followed by a comma [5].

questions.question: Which marker introduces an Adverb Clause of Concession?
questions.explanation: Markers like 'although', 'though', and 'even though' introduce concession clauses [2].

questions.question: What is the function of 'unless' in an adverb clause?
questions.explanation: 'Unless' is a subordinator for clauses of condition [2].

questions.question: Identify the type (Activity 2): 'Whether you join the picnic or not, you will have to pay.'
questions.explanation: 'Whether... or not' is listed as a marker for clauses of concession [2, 4].

questions.question: Match the clauses (Activity 3): 'If you heat iron, ________.'
questions.explanation: Scientific fact: iron expands when heated [6].

questions.question: Identify the third conditional (unfulfilled past):

options.A: If you study hard, you will pass.
options.B: If you studied hard, you would pass.
options.C: If you had studied hard, you would have passed.

questions.explanation: The third conditional uses Past Perfect in the if-clause and 'would have + en' in the main clause [5].

questions.question: Which of these is an Adverb Clause of Manner subordinator?
questions.explanation: 'As if' and 'as though' are common markers for manner [2].

questions.question: In 'The sum is not as difficult as I thought', the clause is one of ________.
questions.explanation: 'As...as' and 'than' are markers for adverb clauses of comparison [2].

questions.question: Complete the imaginary condition (Activity 4): 'Had I the wings of a dove, I ________.'
questions.explanation: This is an inverted second conditional (If I had...), requiring 'would' in the main clause [5, 7].

questions.question: Identify the type (Activity 5): 'The day is quite warm because the sun is shining.'
questions.explanation: It has one main clause and one subordinate adverb clause of reason [8].

questions.question: Which marker is found in 'Adverb Clause of Place'?
questions.explanation: 'Wherever' indicates location/place [2].

questions.question: Identify the adverb clause: 'I am taking an umbrella in case it rains.'
questions.explanation: 'In case' functions as a subordinator of condition [2, 9].

questions.question: Complete the scientific truth: 'If you boil water up to 100°C, it ________.'
questions.explanation: Zero conditional (facts) uses present simple in both parts [3, 7].

questions.question: Identify the type (Activity 2): 'Provided you are in need, we shall help you.'
questions.explanation: 'Provided' is a marker of condition [2, 10].

questions.question: Which of these expresses an unfulfilled condition in the past?

options.A: If he comes, I shall go.
options.B: If he came, I would go.
options.C: If he had come, I would have gone.
options.D: Unless he comes, I won't go.

questions.explanation: The third conditional represents conditions that were not fulfilled in the past [11].

questions.question: Identify the adverb clause marker of time that suggests 'immediately after':
questions.explanation: 'As soon as' is a marker for time meaning immediately following an event [2].

questions.question: In 'They answered better than I had hoped', the clause is one of ________.
questions.explanation: The use of 'better than' indicates comparison [2, 9].

questions.question: Which marker is NOT used for clauses of Condition?
questions.explanation: 'Whenever' is used for clauses of time, not condition [2].

questions.question: Match (Activity 3): 'If you pour oil on water, ________.'
questions.explanation: Scientific truth: oil floats on water [6].

questions.question: Identify the type: 'Although he went to the market, he forgot to buy the vegetables.'
questions.explanation: 'Although' introduces a concession (a contrast to the main clause) [4].

questions.question: What is the inverted form of 'If wishes were horses'?
questions.explanation: Conditionals can be inverted by removing 'if' and placing the auxiliary at the start [10].

questions.question: Identify the type (Activity 5): 'Waste not, want not.'
questions.explanation: This is a compound sentence (equivalent to: 'If you waste not, you will want not') [9].

questions.question: Which marker introduces a clause of Reason?
questions.explanation: 'Since', 'as', and 'because' can all introduce reasons [2].

questions.question: Complete the imaginary condition: 'If I were you, I ________.'
questions.explanation: Second conditional (imaginary) uses 'would' in the main clause [5, 7].

questions.question: Identify the clause type: 'Make hay while the sun shines.'
questions.explanation: 'While the sun shines' refers to time [2, 8].

questions.question: Which of these subordinators shows 'manner'?
questions.explanation: 'As though' is a marker for adverb clauses of manner [2].

questions.question: What tense is used in the main clause of a first conditional (50-50 chance)?
questions.explanation: First conditional uses the present in the if-clause and future (will/shall) in the main clause [3, 5].

questions.question: Identify the type (Activity 2): 'As the dog returns to his vomit, so a fool returns to his folly.'
questions.explanation: This biblical proverb uses 'as...so' to describe the manner of returning [4].

questions.question: Match (Activity 3): 'If you had not reminded me, ________.'

options.B: I could have arrived there on time

questions.explanation: Third conditional requires 'could have + en' in the main clause [5, 6].

questions.question: Adverb clauses of 'concession' are also known as clauses of ________.
questions.explanation: Concession clauses (although, though) introduce information that contrasts with the main clause [12].

questions.question: Which of these is a markers of an Adverb Clause of Time?
questions.explanation: 'As soon as' is used to show a time relationship [2].

questions.question: Identify the clause type: 'I met him as I was leaving the house.'
questions.explanation: 'As' here means 'at the time that' (when) [2, 9].

questions.question: Identify the adverb clause marker of place:
questions.explanation: 'Wherever' is a subordinator for place [2].

questions.question: Complete (Activity 1): 'The sum is not as difficult ________.'
questions.explanation: Adverb clause of comparison uses the 'as (adj) as' structure [13].

questions.question: What is 'third conditional' used for?
questions.explanation: It describes a past condition that was not met, meaning the result was not achieved [11].

questions.question: Identify the type (Activity 2): 'We went out to play as the weather became better.'
questions.explanation: Activity 2 identifies this 'as' clause as one of time [4].

questions.question: Which marker introduces a clause of purpose?
questions.explanation: 'So that' or 'in order that' show the goal or purpose of an action [2].

questions.question: Match (Activity 3): 'If the sun sets, ________.'
questions.explanation: Fact: it becomes dark after the sun sets [6].

questions.question: Identify the type: 'The Pupils worked harder than we had thought.'
questions.explanation: 'Than we had thought' compares the actual work to expectation [2, 12].

questions.question: Which conditional uses 'would have + past participle' in the main clause?
questions.explanation: This structure is specific to the third conditional [5].

questions.question: Which of these markers shows 'reason'?
questions.explanation: 'As' can introduce clauses of time or reason [2].

questions.question: Identify the adverb clause (Activity 6): 'I am sorry that he has been disturbed.'
questions.explanation: This clause explains the reason for being sorry [9, 14].

questions.question: Identify the marker in 'No matter how hard he tries, he fails':
questions.explanation: 'No matter how' is a subordinator of concession.
